diagrams.title: Process Structure with Critical Section
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a process divided into Entry, Critical Section, Exit, and Remainder sections
elements: Remainder Section (no request to enter CS)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the organization of code in critical section problem solutions and where synchronization primitives are placed

diagrams.title: Peterson's Algorithm for Two Processes
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how two processes coordinate using turn variable and flag array
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the synchronization mechanism in Peterson's solution and understanding how mutual exclusion is achieved

diagrams.title: TestAndSet Lock Implementation
diagrams.description: Shows how TestAndSet atomic instruction is used to implement a simple lock mechanism
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hardware-based mutual exclusion implementation and atomic operations in practice

diagrams.title: Race Condition Example
diagrams.description: Illustrates how race condition occurs when two processes concurrently modify shared variable without synchronization
elements: Correct sequential execution showing correct result
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why critical section problem exists and the consequences of improper synchronization
